ticket.subject: Notification.
ticket.description:
Hi, 
Please see below for an email received from you guys. Can you please help direct me on how to do this so I don't run into disruptions for my business moving forward? 

| Hi there, |
| We're reaching out with an important update that directly affects your FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label. FedEx has officially announced the retirement of its legacy Web Services (SOAP) APIs, and all merchants using FedEx for shipping must migrate to FedEx REST APIs to avoid disruption. |
| As a FedEx Certified Compatible Solution Provider, the FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label is fully prepared for REST APIs. We've already completed verification and production readiness — so the transition on your end is straightforward. |


| What's Happening |
| FedEx is permanently retiring its Web Services platform. If you're currently using legacy Web Services credentials (meter number, authentication key, etc.) in your FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label, those credentials will stop working according to the following timeline: |


| 
| Migration Timeline |
| 
| • | March 31, 2026 — Final provider API verification & production readiness deadline |

 |
| 
| • | April 1, 2026 — Meter deactivation window begins; Web Services retirement starts |

 |
| 
| • | June 1, 2026 — Final Web Services shutdown (all legacy endpoints permanently offline) |

 |

 |


| What This Means for You |
| If no action is taken, your FedEx shipping rates, label printing, and tracking functionality within the plugin may be affected after April 1, 2026. This could impact your ability to display shipping rates, print FedEx labels, and fulfill orders from your WooCommerce store. |


| How to Migrate (3 Simple Steps) |


| 
| 1 |

 | Register for FedEx REST APIs
Complete the registration directly from within the plugin settings. You'll need to provide your account and contact details — name, company name, phone number, email, and the complete business address associated with your FedEx account. |


|   | Validate Your FedEx Account
Choose a verification method to confirm your identity:
| • OTP via SMS |
| • OTP via Phone Call |
| • OTP via Email |
| • Invoice |
| • Help Desk |

 |


| 
| 2 |

 | Credentials Configured Automatically
Once registration and validation are complete, your new REST API credentials will be configured automatically within the plugin. No manual key entry required. |


| 
| 3 |

 | Test & Confirm
Run a test shipment or rate request to verify that shipping rates display correctly and label printing works as expected with the new REST API credentials. |



Thank you,Duong Pham | Manager Sunshine Nail & Beauty Supply, LLC
4833 South 72nd Street Omaha, NE 68127 Tel: (402) 884-5972

comments.body:
Hi,



Please see below for an email received from you guys. Can you please help direct me on how to do this so I don't run into disruptions for my business moving forward?



Hi there,
We're reaching out with an important update that directly affects your **FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label**. **FedEx has officially announced the retirement of its legacy Web Services (SOAP) APIs**, and all merchants using FedEx for shipping must migrate to FedEx REST APIs to avoid disruption.
**As a FedEx Certified Compatible Solution Provider, the FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label is fully prepared for REST APIs.** We've already completed verification and production readiness — so the transition on your end is straightforward.

What's Happening
FedEx is permanently retiring its Web Services platform. If you're currently using legacy Web Services credentials (meter number, authentication key, etc.) in your FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label, those credentials will stop working according to the following timeline:

Migration Timeline

• **March 31, 2026** — Final provider API verification & production readiness deadline

• **April 1, 2026** — Meter deactivation window begins; Web Services retirement starts

• **June 1, 2026** — Final Web Services shutdown (all legacy endpoints permanently offline)

What This Means for You
If no action is taken, **your FedEx shipping rates, label printing, and tracking functionality within the plugin may be affected after April 1, 2026.** This could impact your ability to display shipping rates, print FedEx labels, and fulfill orders from your WooCommerce store.

How to Migrate (3 Simple Steps)

1 **Register for FedEx REST APIs**
Complete the registration directly from within the plugin settings. You'll need to provide your account and contact details — name, company name, phone number, email, and the complete business address associated with your FedEx account.

  **Validate Your FedEx Account**
Choose a verification method to confirm your identity:

• OTP via SMS
• OTP via Phone Call
• OTP via Email
• Invoice
• Help Desk

2 **Credentials Configured Automatically**
Once registration and validation are complete, your new REST API credentials will be configured automatically within the plugin. No manual key entry required.

3 **Test & Confirm**
Run a test shipment or rate request to verify that shipping rates display correctly and label printing works as expected with the new REST API credentials.





Thank you,
Duong Pham | Manager
Sunshine Nail & Beauty Supply, LLC

4833 South 72nd Street

Omaha, NE 68127

Tel: (402) 884-5972
comments.html_body:
Hi, 

Please see below for an email received from you guys. Can you please help direct me on how to do this so I don't run into disruptions for my business moving forward? 

Hi there,
We're reaching out with an important update that directly affects your FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label. FedEx has officially announced the retirement of its legacy Web Services (SOAP) APIs, and all merchants using FedEx for shipping must migrate to FedEx REST APIs to avoid disruption.
As a FedEx Certified Compatible Solution Provider, the FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label is fully prepared for REST APIs. We've already completed verification and production readiness — so the transition on your end is straightforward.
What's Happening
FedEx is permanently retiring its Web Services platform. If you're currently using legacy Web Services credentials (meter number, authentication key, etc.) in your FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label, those credentials will stop working according to the following timeline:
Migration Timeline
•March 31, 2026 — Final provider API verification & production readiness deadline
•April 1, 2026 — Meter deactivation window begins; Web Services retirement starts
•June 1, 2026 — Final Web Services shutdown (all legacy endpoints permanently offline)
What This Means for You
If no action is taken, your FedEx shipping rates, label printing, and tracking functionality within the plugin may be affected after April 1, 2026. This could impact your ability to display shipping rates, print FedEx labels, and fulfill orders from your WooCommerce store.
How to Migrate (3 Simple Steps)
1
Register for FedEx REST APIs
Complete the registration directly from within the plugin settings. You'll need to provide your account and contact details — name, company name, phone number, email, and the complete business address associated with your FedEx account.
 Validate Your FedEx Account
Choose a verification method to confirm your identity:
• OTP via SMS
• OTP via Phone Call
• OTP via Email
• Invoice
• Help Desk
2
Credentials Configured Automatically
Once registration and validation are complete, your new REST API credentials will be configured automatically within the plugin. No manual key entry required.
3
Test & Confirm
Run a test shipment or rate request to verify that shipping rates display correctly and label printing works as expected with the new REST API credentials.


Thank you,
Duong Pham | Manager 
Sunshine Nail & Beauty Supply, LLC
4833 South 72nd Street 
Omaha, NE 68127 
Tel: (402) 884-5972

system.client: WebService/1.1.25198 YMailNorrin

comments.body:
Hello Duong Pham,

Thank you for reaching out to us.
Rest assured, we are here to assist you.

Before we proceed, we noticed that you do not have an active license for the **FedEx Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label**, and the FedEx plugin has already been refunded.

Could you please clarify which solution you are referring to?

Once we have this information, we’ll be able to assist you accordingly.

Looking forward to your response.

Regards,

Shivani Shinde
IT Analyst
Team PluginHive
https://www.pluginhive.com

comments.body:
Hello Duong,

Thank you for sharing the details.

The email you received is regarding the official announcement from FedEx about the retirement of their legacy Web Services (SOAP) APIs, which is the method currently used to connect your FedEx account in the plugin.

To avoid any disruption in service after March 31, 2026, it is necessary to migrate your integration to the FedEx REST APIs.

We will be happy to assist you with connecting your FedEx account using the REST API. To proceed, please schedule a meeting using the appointment link below:
[Schedule an appointment]

If you do not see any available slots at the moment, please check again over the weekend. The slots for next Monday should become available by then, and you can book a suitable time.

Looking forward to assisting you.

Best regards,
Sibi Firoz
Team PluginHive
comments.html_body:
Hello Duong,
 
Thank you for sharing the details.
 
The email you received is regarding the official announcement from FedEx about the retirement of their legacy Web Services (SOAP) APIs, which is the method currently used to connect your FedEx account in the plugin.
 
To avoid any disruption in service after March 31, 2026, it is necessary to migrate your integration to the FedEx REST APIs.
 
We will be happy to assist you with connecting your FedEx account using the REST API. To proceed, please schedule a meeting using the appointment link below:
[Schedule an appointment]
 
If you do not see any available slots at the moment, please check again over the weekend. The slots for next Monday should become available by then, and you can book a suitable time.
 
Looking forward to assisting you.
 
Best regards,
Sibi Firoz
Team PluginHive

Request #378276 "Give the WooCommerce Shipping Se..." was closed and merged into this request. Last comment in request #378276:

Hello Omaha SEO,

 We regret that you had to uninstall our **WooCommerce Shipping Services Plugin** from https://test1.ssnailsupply.com

 If you have faced any difficulties with the plugin, we can hop on a call to assist you. Or if you are awaiting your WooCommerce store launch, do let us know so we can extend your free trial period.


> **Did you know?**
>
> - _Our shipping plugin is certified by top shipping carriers like UPS, FedEx, USPS, Stamps.com, DHL Express, Australia Post, Canada Post, Post Nord & many more._
> - _This plugin will allow you to ship with multiple carrier(s)._
> - _Print 100 (s) of shipping labels in a single click._
> - _Our shipping experts will provide you with quick and reliable support._



 Let’s schedule a quick call to discuss how exactly we can help you reach your shipping requirements.

 We would like to hear from you.

 Thanks and Regards,
 Team PluginHive
https://www.pluginhive.com/about/#team

comments.body:
Call summary:

Assisted the customer with migrating to FedEx Rest API - migration completed smoothly with invoice validation type

Also guided on updating automation rules and packaging settings.

On call we reprocessed orders and confirmed rates are working as expected

Customer had few queries related to tracking and order importing and how product will be synced - addressed

Customer was happy and requested for review
comments.html_body:
Call summary:
 
Assisted the customer with migrating to FedEx Rest API - migration completed smoothly with invoice validation type
 
Also guided on updating automation rules and packaging settings.
 
On call we reprocessed orders and confirmed rates are working as expected
 
Customer had few queries related to tracking and order importing and how product will be synced - addressed
 
Customer was happy and requested for review

comments.body:
Hi,



Please see the attached screenshot for reference.

Initially, I thought the pricing discrepancy was due to the **declared value** of each shipment, since the default is set at **$100 per package**. However, I never experienced this issue with the previous FedEx connection.

Today, I noticed a significant increase in shipping costs, so I ran a comparison using the same **$100 declared value**. The pricing between the **PluginHive system and the FedEx website is noticeably different**, which is concerning.

Could you please look into this and let me know what’s causing the discrepancy? This is affecting our shipping costs, so I’d appreciate a prompt response.





Thank you,
Duong Pham | Manager
Sunshine Nail & Beauty Supply, LLC

4833 South 72nd Street

Omaha, NE 68127

Tel: (402) 884-5972

comments.body:
L3 - Need assistance with two cases

- New Edit Package feature not available for the FedEx Rest API account. Please refer to the recording shared below for more details

- For the FedEx shipping rates, the customer found a discrpancy with the rates shown through the app and on the FedEx portal. For FedEx 2 Day, the shipping cost shown on the FedEx portal is $11.93  and in the plugin is $16.88
There is a surcharge of $4.95 applied by FedEx which is causing the rate difference. This surcharge is related to a declared value (refer to the Request Response XML attached).
In the request, the customs value of the product is passed which might be causing to add a surcharge. FYI the order used for checking this case is a Domestic order.
There is no settings enabled in the plugin related to the insurance, so not sure from where this surcharge is getting added.
Please refer to the video recording link shared and the Request Response XMLs attached for better understanding of the case.


Video Recording Link - https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VchxU2YmqLNO7jN4i7xkKLrBUlagORiE/view?usp=sharing

Please check and let us know of the updates

Store URL - https://ssnailsupply.com
Account ID - 97b76d06-a9a5-4bcf-9d77-b20bce1f99b2
comments.html_body:
L3 - Need assistance with two cases
 
  • New Edit Package feature not available for the FedEx Rest API account. Please refer to the recording shared below for more details
     
  • For the FedEx shipping rates, the customer found a discrpancy with the rates shown through the app and on the FedEx portal. For FedEx 2 Day, the shipping cost shown on the FedEx portal is $11.93  and in the plugin is $16.88 
    There is a surcharge of $4.95 applied by FedEx which is causing the rate difference. This surcharge is related to a declared value (refer to the Request Response XML attached). 
    In the request, the customs value of the product is passed which might be causing to add a surcharge. FYI the order used for checking this case is a Domestic order.
    There is no settings enabled in the plugin related to the insurance, so not sure from where this surcharge is getting added.
    Please refer to the video recording link shared and the Request Response XMLs attached for better understanding of the case.

comments.body:
Moving back to L3 -

1. Understood. The requirement has to go to Dev_Needed

2. In the plugin, there is no settings related to insurance enabled and the product associated with the order does not have any customs value provided. In the request we are passing customs value for a domestic shipment?
Since the customs value or declared value is needed for the insurance and in this case since customer does not need the packages to be insured, we would like to know why and where this surcharge is getting calculated?

However, the customer wants to see the actual one rate of $11.93 and not $16.88 as the customer does not wants the insurance applied for the domestic shipments.

So, the case here is about the customs value passed in the request which is not required for the customer.

Thanks and Regards,
